Hero.headline: Code as You Think.
Hero.description: Hüma is a high-performance programming language featuring native Turkish syntax. Built with a Rust-backed core, it combines the expressiveness of natural language with the raw speed of a systems compiler.

natural_syntax.title: Natural Syntax
natural_syntax.description: Write algorithms in your native tongue. Reduce cognitive load and increase maintainability with Turkish semantic structures that translate directly into machine code.

hybrid_execution.title: Hybrid Execution
hybrid_execution.description: Interpret for rapid prototyping or compile for production. Hüma utilizes a multi-stage JIT and AOT pipeline powered by the LLVM infrastructure.

standalone_compilation.title: Standalone Compilation
standalone_compilation.description: Zero dependencies. Compile your Hüma projects into standalone binaries for Windows, Linux, and macOS with optimized memory management.

standard_library.title: Rich Standard Library
standard_library.description: A robust set of modules for networking, file I/O, and data processing. Designed to be batteries-included while staying lightweight.

CTA.headline: Ready to evolve?
CTA.description: Join thousands of developers bridging the gap between human thought and binary execution.

Footer.copy: © 2026 Hüma Language Foundation. Kinetic Archive Edition.

Playground.headline: Pure Efficiency.
Playground.description: Discover the Hüma language. Programming is now much more accessible and powerful with its syntax close to daily spoken language.
Playground.cta_title: The Most Natural Way to Code.
Playground.cta_desc: To use all the features of Hüma, install the CLI tool on your computer and start developing your own software.

hello.label: Welcome to Hüma
hello.description: A basic example of variable assignment and printing in Hüma. The simplest structure that utilizes the natural flow of the Turkish language.

conditions.label: Decision Mechanisms
conditions.description: Flow control with ise / yoksa blocks. You can build conditional statements with the 'ise' keyword.

loops.label: Iterative Operations
loops.description: The 'olduğu sürece' structure is the direct equivalent of the classic while loop.

functions.label: Function Definition
functions.description: Functions are defined with the 'olsun' and 'alsın' structure, and return values with 'döndür'.

classes.label: Object-Oriented Programming
classes.description: Group your data and behaviors (functions) using classes and object structures.

lists.label: List Management
lists.description: Add elements, remove them, and check length with Hüma's dynamic list structure.

Community.headline: Build Hüma.
Community.description: Hüma is a community-driven language. Every issue opened, every PR merged, and every discussion started shapes what Hüma becomes. Join us on GitHub.
Community.contributing_desc: Hüma is written in Rust. You'll need cargo installed. Here's how to get from zero to a merged PR:
Community.star_desc: Every star helps Hüma reach more developers. It takes two seconds and means everything for an indie language project.

repo.title: GitHub Repository
repo.description: Browse the source code, open issues, and contribute pull requests. All development happens in the open.
repo.label: View on GitHub

bugs.title: Bug Reports
bugs.description: Found a bug in the interpreter or compiler? Open a detailed issue on GitHub with a reproducible example.
bugs.label: Report a Bug

features.title: Feature Requests
features.description: Have an idea for a new keyword, stdlib module, or toolchain feature? Open a discussion and let's build it together.
features.label: Start a Discussion

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.description: See every version update, breaking change, and new feature since the initial release of Hüma.
changelog.label: View Changelog

IDE_Page.headline: The Official
IDE_Page.description: A native, cross-platform desktop application powered by Tauri and React. Experience zero-latency editing with Monaco and an integrated xterm.js terminal.

monaco.title: Monaco Editor
monaco.desc: Powered by the same core as VS Code. Get full semantic highlighting, minimap support, and multi-cursor editing out of the box.

terminal.title: xterm.js Integration
terminal.desc: An embedded native-feeling PTY terminal. Run hüma interactively, execute scripts, and view diagnostics directly from the bottom panel.

tauri.title: Tauri Native Core
tauri.desc: Built in Rust using Tauri. Lightweight, fast, and secure. Native file system access and system dialogs without the heavy Chromium overhead.

getting_started.title: Getting Started
getting_started.description: Install Hüma and write your first program in minutes. Complete getting-started guide.
getting_started.hero_desc: Welcome to Hüma — a high-performance language designed for precision and kinetic speed. This guide will help you set up your environment and write your first Hüma program.

installation.title: Installation
installation.desc: The Hüma toolchain is distributed as a single static binary. Use our shell script to automatically detect your architecture and install the huma-cli.
installation.arch_desc: Hüma currently supports x86_64 and ARM64 architectures natively. For legacy systems, please refer to the compilation-from-source documentation.

quick_start.title: Quick Start
quick_start.desc: Verify your installation by checking the version. The Hüma compiler should respond instantly, reflecting our commitment to zero-latency developer tools.

first_program.title: First Program
first_program.desc: Hüma syntax is designed to be expressive yet rigid. Below is a classic conditional block demonstrating our core keywords: olsun (let), ise (if), and yoksa (else).
first_program.suffix_desc: Hüma uses Turkish grammatical suffixes with an apostrophe (') as a separator. The lexer automatically strips suffixes like 'yı, 'i, and 'nı — they are grammatical decoration, not code.
